title: Mazars Comment: Sticky growth or a Wiley E. Consumer Moment?
section_path: Mazars Comment: Sticky growth or a Wiley E. Consumer Moment?
section_path_text: Mazars Comment: Sticky growth or a Wiley E. Consumer Moment?
summary:
Main
By George Lagarias
Before children's cartoons turned philosophical, opting to promote role-modelling and self-actualisation, they were mostly about slapstick comedy and serious self, or otherwise-inflicted, injury. Back in those dark days of exposing the souls of the innocent to wanton violence, one of the most notorious villains was Wiley E. Aptly named, the coyote was engaged in the perpetual pursuit of a Roadrunner -a bird that can go up to 20 miles (43 kilometres) per hour - yes, that's a real thing.
